title_en: Introduction to Bada'i as-Sana'i by al-Kasani
content_en:
Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib ash-Shara'i' ('The Masterworks of Craftsmanship in Ordering the Laws') is considered the most elegantly organized and clearly reasoned work of classical Hanafi jurisprudence, authored by 'Ala' ad-Din Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud ibn Ahmad al-Kasani al-Hanafi (d. 587 AH / 1191 CE). It stands apart from other Hanafi encyclopedias through its logical organization, clear prose, and the distinctive care with which it grounds every ruling in its evidential and rational basis.

Al-Kasani was a Central Asian scholar who studied at Samarqand and became one of the leading Hanafi authorities of his era. He composed Bada'i as-Sana'i partly as an elaboration of Tuhfat al-Fuqaha' by his teacher 'Ala' ad-Din as-Samarqandi (d. 539 AH), and the tradition reports that as-Samarqandi was so impressed by the work that he gave al-Kasani his daughter in marriage as her mahr — a testament to the scholarly excellence of the composition.

The organization of Bada'i as-Sana'i is one of its most celebrated features. Al-Kasani arranges each legal chapter according to a systematic framework: he first presents the general rulings, then discusses the conditions (shurut), then the elements that constitute the ruling (arkan), then the types of the ruling, then its consequences, and finally its nullifiers. This consistent organizational pattern — applied to every significant legal topic — makes the work remarkably easy to navigate compared to many classical fiqh texts.

The clarity of al-Kasani's prose is another distinguishing feature. He writes in clear, elegant Arabic that explains the reasoning behind each ruling — not merely stating the rule but explaining why the Hanafi school reaches this conclusion, what evidential considerations drive it, and how it is superior to the alternatives proposed by other schools. This explanatory quality makes Bada'i as-Sana'i both a legal reference and a work of legal education.

Bada'i as-Sana'i spans seven large volumes in printed editions and covers the full range of Islamic law. It is considered one of the 'six major works' of the classical Hanafi school alongside al-Hidayah, al-Mabsut, Al-Muhit, al-Fatawa al-'Alamgiriyyah, and Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq. Together these works constitute the foundational library of advanced Hanafi scholarship.

The work's influence extends to modern Islamic scholarship. Its clear organization and principled reasoning have made it a frequent citation in contemporary Islamic finance literature, where scholars seek the Hanafi school's framework for evaluating novel financial instruments.
content_id:
Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib asy-Syara'i ('Karya Agung Kerajinan dalam Mengatur Hukum-Hukum') dianggap sebagai karya fikih Hanafi klasik yang paling elegan dalam susunannya dan paling jelas dalam penalarannya. Pengarangnya, Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani (wafat 587 H / 1191 M), belajar di bawah bimbingan 'Ala ad-Din as-Samarqandi dan menikahi putri gurunya — sebuah detail yang secara metaforis mencerminkan hubungan dekat karya Bada'i dengan Tuhfat al-Fuqaha karya gurunya, yang menjadi basis teks sekaligus dilampaui secara menyeluruh oleh karya al-Kasani.

Prinsip pengorganisasian yang membedakan Bada'i adalah apa yang disebut al-Kasani sendiri dengan tartib — penyusunan hukum-hukum ilahi menurut kerangka analitis yang sistematis. Untuk setiap institusi hukum, al-Kasani secara eksplisit mengidentifikasi syarat-syaratnya (syuruth), rukun-rukunnya (arkan), faktor-faktor yang membatalkannya (nawaqidh), dan pertanyaan-pertanyaan cabangnya (furu'). Kerangka empat bagian ini bukan sekadar perangkat pedagogis melainkan mencerminkan cara al-Kasani memahami struktur hukum Islam itu sendiri: setiap institusi memiliki bentuk yang dapat dianalisis, dan memahami bentuk itu adalah memahami institusinya.

Madzhab Hanafi telah mengakui Bada'i as-Sana'i sebagai salah satu teks referensi intinya sejak lama. Dalam kurikulum hukum Hanafi yang dikodifikasi di madrasah-madrasah Ottoman dan dipraktikkan dalam sistem pendidikan Islam tradisional di Asia Selatan dan Asia Tengah, Bada'i menempati posisi kanonik bersama At-Tuhfah, Al-Hidayah, Al-Mabsuth, dan Radd al-Muhtar. Posisi ini mencerminkan penilaian tradisi bahwa al-Kasani mencapai sesuatu yang unik: sebuah penyajian komprehensif dari hukum Hanafi yang jernih secara analitis sekaligus dapat diakses secara pedagogis.
